ops.note: Wave 4 trust tests + cross-team fixture landed on daemonkit main (test/trust commit 47565e2), all GREEN via scripts/test.sh. DELIVERED: (fork-exec) TF1 live non-vacuous pin — a same-UID peer execs /bin/sleep on the same pid+socket and the LOCAL_PEERTOKEN identity flips (TestPeerTokenIsQueryTimeLive_TF1 ungated + TestPolicyCheckAcceptsSubstitutedPeer_TF1 E2E-gated); (cross-team reject) TestTrustRejectsWrongTeam — a Requirement naming a non-matching team is refused via the DR leaf OU clause. HONEST GAPS (no vacuous green): (a) TF1/TF2/TF5 are DOCUMENTED-LIMITATION tests, not rejection tests — the E1 spike proved LOCAL_PEERTOKEN is query-time-live so the unix transport CANNOT reject same-UID fork/exec/PID-reuse/fd-delegation; they flip to rejection tests only against the future XPC transport (db24393). TF2 (PID reuse) + TF5 (fd delegation) are t.Skip (nondeterministic / privileged harness). (b) the cross-team fixture is the INVERSE — it proves our DR rejects a non-matching team, NOT that a genuine team-B Developer-ID binary is rejected; minting a real second Developer ID team is not possible in this env. Fork-bomb-safe: the re-exec helper is TestMain-guarded, the substitution target is always a foreign image, never os.Executable().
